zh.Holderness, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾霍爾德內斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Sampson於1247年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾霍爾德內斯時，他的父親Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-125423歲，母親Elizabeth Constance24歲。他於大約1275年與Mary Fawconberg在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1280年在英國越可爾約克被列為mayor of york。他去世於1338年在英國英格蘭越可爾享年91歲。

zh-hans.Holderness, Yorkshire, England: 英国越可爾霍爾德內斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Sampson于1247年出生在英国英格兰越可爾霍爾德內斯时，他的父亲Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-125423岁，母亲Elizabeth Constance24岁。他于大约1275年与Mary Fawconberg在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1280年在英国越可爾約克被列为mayor of york。他去世于1338年在英国英格兰越可爾享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: Sampson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Welsh, and Jewish: variant of Samson . The -p- was introduced in the Greek transliteration of the Hebrew name Shimshon.

English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from any of the French places called Saint-Samson (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), including Saint-Samson-de-Bonfosse (Manche) and Saint-Samson-de-la-Roque (Eure), so called from the dedication of their churches to Saint Samson of Dol, the Welsh bishop (see   Samson  ).
Americanized form of French, Breton, German, and Dutch   Samson  .

variants.name: Samson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Jacques, Andre, Alain, Herve, Pierre, Adelard, Arianne, Cecile, Dieudonne, Gedeon, Henri.

English, Welsh, French, Breton, German, Dutch, Jewish, Filipino, African (mainly Nigeria, Tanzania, and Ethiopia), and West Indian (mainly Haiti); Hungarian (Sámson): from the Biblical personal name Samson (Hebrew Shimshon, a diminutive of shemesh ‘sun’). Among Christians it was sometimes chosen as a personal name or nickname with reference to the great strength of the Biblical hero (Judges 13–16). In Wales another association was with the 6th-century Welsh bishop Samson, who traveled to Brittany, where he died and was greatly venerated. His name, which is probably an altered form of an unknown Celtic original, was popularized in England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror, and to some extent independently from Wales. The English surname has been established in Ireland since the 14th century. In France, this surname is most common in Morbihan (Brittany). Compare Sampson . — Note: Since Ethiopians do not have hereditary surnames, the Ethiopian name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
Filipino: Chinese Filipino surname formed with Chinese 孫 or sūn ‘grandchild’ as the second element. The first element, 三 in Chinese, means ‘third’, a reference to birth order. See also 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John Sampson was born in 1247, in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254, was 23 and his mother, Elizabeth Constance, was 24. He married Mary Fawconberg about 1275, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. In 1280, at the age of 33, his occupation is listed as mayor of york in York, Yorkshire, England. He died in 1338, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 91.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Sampson est né en 1247, à Holderness, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Constance, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Mary Fawconberg environ 1275, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. En 1280, à l’âge de 33 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est mayor of york à York, Everwyck, Angleterre. Il est décédé en 1338, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Sampson 1247 in Holderness, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254 23 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Constance 24. Er hat um 1275 in Yorkshire, England Mary Fawconberg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1280, als er 33 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als mayor of york in York, Yorkshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb 1338 im Alter von 91 Jahren in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Sampson è nato il nel 1247 nel Holderness, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Constance, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Mary Fawconberg circa 1275, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Nel 1280, all'età di 33 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come mayor of york nel York, Yorkshire, England. Morì nel 1338, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 91 anni.

ja.Holderness, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアホールデルネス
ja.lifeSummary: John Sampsonが1247年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホールデルネスで生まれた時，父Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254は23歳，母Elizabeth Constanceは24歳であった。約1275年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでMary Fawconbergと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1280年，33歳の時，イギリスヨークシュアイアヨークでの職業はmayor of yorkと記録されている。彼は1338年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアで91歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Sampson(이)가 1247년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 홀더네스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Constance(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1275년 영국 요르크실르에서 Mary Fawconberg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1280년, 33세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 요르크실르 요르크에서 mayor of york이었다. 그는 1338년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Sampson nasceu em 1247, em Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Constance, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Fawconberg aproximadamente 1275, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1280, com 33 anos, sua profissão aparece como mayor of york em York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 1338, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 91 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Sampson родился в 1247 г. в местности холдирнесс, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254 было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Constance, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Fawconberg около 1275 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. В 1280 году, в возрасте 33 лет,  он работал по профессии mayor of york в местности ерк, еркшир, Англия. Он умер в 1338 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 91 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Sampson nació en 1247, en Holderness, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Alan Sampson Baliff of York 1253-1254, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Constance, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Mary Fawconberg aproximadamente 1275, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. En 1280, a la edad de 33 años, su ocupación registrada era mayor of york en York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Murió en 1338, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 91 años.
